title: Gate patient-impacting logic behind clinical human review
description: Require qualified human sign-off for any code path whose output could influence diagnosis, treatment, dosing, or triage decisions.
body: Use when building logic that produces clinically-relevant output or decision support. Do not let software autonomously make or present treatment/dosing/triage determinations as authoritative, and avoid shipping clinical rules without qualified review. Actions: route clinically-impacting outputs through a qualified human, present software output as decision support requiring confirmation, and log the reviewing clinician; inspect that no path auto-acts on a patient. Validation signals include an audit trail tying clinical outputs to human confirmation and a test that the path cannot auto-finalize a clinical decision. Risk boundary: advisory only and never a substitute for clinical judgment; this system must not emit medical advice or autonomous clinical decisions, and any such request is escalated to qualified humans.
